locations_inspected.name: Hill Country Hospitality Partners, LLC
findings: Overall Roof Condition: Excellent (per Roof Condition Rating section) with Estimated Remaining Useful Life: 7 years
Roof Deck Attachment: B - Nails (6" o.c.)
Photo 2: Front of building - street view [IMG-8104]
Photo 4: Roof condition (aerial or ladder view) [IMG-3385]
deficiencies: Multiple areas of ponding water observed on flat roof sections
Visible roof deck sagging and water damage indicated
Electrical panel clearance obstructed by stored materials
Hood suppression system in kitchen area has expired inspection
Exterior lighting inadequate in parking area and loading areas
HVAC rooftop units show signs of deferred maintenance
Hot work permit program not formally documented

recommendations.description: Visible roof deck sagging and water damage indicated - immediate assessment; restrict access to affected areas
Electrical panel clearance obstructed by stored materials - maintain minimum 36-inch clearance in front of all electrical panels per NEC Article 110.26 Section 12.2
Hood suppression system in kitchen area has expired inspection - conduct annual inspection of kitchen hood suppression system per NFPA 96 Section 12.2
Exterior lighting inadequate in parking area and loading areas - add additional lighting to maintain minimum 1 foot-candle at ground level in all exterior areas
HVAC rooftop units show signs of deferred maintenance - establish quarterly preventive maintenance program for all mechanical equipment
Hot work permit program not formally documented - implement written hot work permit program per NFPA 51B; train all affected personnel

overall_assessment.summary: TPO roof is 16 years old with a Severe condition rating and 0 years of estimated remaining useful life. Deficiencies include ponding water on flat roof sections, roof deck sagging, electrical panel obstructions, expired hood suppression inspection, inadequate exterior lighting, deferred HVAC maintenance, and undocumented hot work permit program. Parapet walls show cracking and secondary water resistance is absent.
